summary: Pristine audience (FOB) recording captures a stellar 1983 Santa Fe show highlighted by a smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s and an explosive Let It Grow.
review: The tape is praised as one of the finest early‑80s audience recordings, with crystal‑clear mids, bright highs and a tight mix that lets every instrument shine. Musically the concert is a fan favorite – the Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence, Let It Grow, Wang Dang Doodle and the ensuing jams are repeatedly called “smoking” or “explosive,” while the band’s overall cohesion and energy are lauded.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (10 out of 12) award 4‑5 stars, extolling both the superb audience recording quality and the standout performances; only technical questions receive low or zero stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Praised as a pristine FOB tape with crisp drums, deep bass, bright keyboards; some note an overly bright sound that may stem from pre‑emphasis not being removed, but overall the mix is considered top‑notch.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a tight, energetic set with the second half propelled by a powerful Rhythm Devils groove; transitions such as Help‑Slip‑Frank and the Let It Grow jam are highlighted as seamless and exhilarating.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is praised as perfect/smoking; voice described as weathered but present, with a slight off‑key start noted by one reviewer.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work is singled out as excellent, especially with the new Modulus six‑string adding depth.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards receive compliments for harmonic richness and crispness; deep tonal quality noted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight drumming praised; two‑drummer dynamic described as hit‑or‑miss in general but tight on this night.
